meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no testing knowledge (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no testing knowledge. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Testing Knowledge
content.seo_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no testing knowledge. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster.
content.answer_block: Stop guessing your pricing and fearing bugs. By integrating Antigravity for automated infrastructure and Claude Code for rapid iterative testing, you replace manual QA with logic-based validation. This workflow ensures your SaaS pricing tiers are mathematically sound, bug-free, and deployed in hours, not weeks, directly increasing your professional output.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank screen, the client's deadline looming like a storm front, and the cold sweat of 'what if this breaks in production' keeps you paralyzed. You lack a formal testing background, and every line of code feels like a gamble. The industry demands perfection, but you just need to ship.

Here is the shift: stop trying to be a QA engineer and start being an architect. We are leveraging AI to build a safety net that catches errors before they reach your users. By automating your pricing logic and testing cycles, you transform from a stressed freelancer into a high-velocity SaaS builder who delivers bulletproof value every single time.

steps.title: Define Tiered Pricing Logic
steps.description: Establish the mathematical boundaries for your SaaS tiers. We need to ensure that logic handles edge cases like upgrades, downgrades, and proration automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust pricing model schema for a SaaS platform with three tiers (Basic, Pro, Enterprise). Include logic for monthly/annual billing cycles and prorated upgrades.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a pricing calculation module with unit tests covering edge cases'

steps.title: Automate Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Since you lack testing knowledge, use AI to generate the test suites based on your pricing logic. This ensures your code behaves exactly as expected under load.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite for the pricing logic module. Focus on boundary conditions like $0 charges, maximum user limits, and leap year billing adjustments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'write unit tests for the pricing module and verify 100% branch coverage'

steps.title: Simulate User Checkout Flows
steps.description: Simulate the end-to-end user experience to ensure that the pricing logic correctly integrates with your payment gateway (e.g., Stripe).
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a simulation script that creates a mock user, upgrades their plan, and verifies the resulting invoice total matches the expected tier calculation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a mock checkout flow script and execute it to validate pricing accuracy'

steps.title: Implement Defensive Error Handling
steps.description: Add layers of protection to your code so that if a calculation fails, the system fails gracefully rather than crashing the user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the pricing controller to include comprehensive error handling and logging. Ensure all failed calculations return a human-readable error and a system admin alert.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor 'add defensive checks and error boundaries to the pricing engine'

steps.title: Deploy with Continuous Integration
steps.description: Connect your automated tests to your deployment pipeline so that no code reaches production unless it passes the pricing logic tests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow that runs our pricing unit tests on every push to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure a CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions to gate deployments on test success'

pro_tips: Always use 'Snapshot Testing' for UI pricing tables to ensure your CSS doesn't break when data changes.
Never hardcode prices; use environment variables or a remote config service to update pricing without redeploying code.
Implement 'Shadow Mode' where you log calculated prices in the background for a week before switching to live billing.
Use Claude Code to generate 'Negative Test Cases'—explicitly ask it to try to break your pricing logic.
Treat your pricing documentation as code; use tools like Swagger to keep your API contracts in sync with your pricing engine.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live and tested, move on to implementing automated user onboarding flows and analytics to track your conversion rate. Continuous refinement is the key to SaaS longevity.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the fear of bugs hold your freelance business back. With this workflow, you can ship professional-grade pricing tiers that scale, impress your clients, and secure your reputation as a top-tier developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
